channel.title: pedagogy on Lisa DeBruine
channel.description: Recent content in pedagogy on Lisa DeBruine

item.title: Solution Feedback
item.description: Download a simple demo .rmd/.html. I’d noticed that students were tending to just look at the hidden solutions and not try very hard to understand the exercises, so I wanted a way for them to check if they’re right, but not give them the answer immediately. I made a quick way to set up an input box that turns blue when you type in the right answer. What is 2 + 2?

item.title: Hidden Solutions
item.description: library(tidyverse) The R for Reproducible Scientific Analysis pages at software carpentry have a really nice interface for hiding and showing solutions to exercises. I’ve created my own lightweight solution that you can use in any html file, including those made by RMarkdown (e.g., R notebooks). Example Graph the relationship between speed and distance for the cars dataset. Solution You can put some text inside the solution, as well as code cunks.
